frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre beta, har aadmi ko shak ki nazar se dekhna bhi theek nahi hai. Do-chaar bure log hote hain, iska matlab yeh thodi na ki sab mard kharab hain; jo seedhe-saadhe ladke hain unko bhi bina baat doshi samjha jata hai. Aur aajkal khatra ladkon ke liye bhi kam nahi hai, isliye itna dar-dar ke rehna bhi samajhdari nahi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering the unfairness supposedly faced by men and minimizing the structural reasons for women's wariness. It encodes the 'not all men' response, generalization policing, and a deflective 'men also face danger' move.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 62. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 21 just told you and the family over dinner that she wants to start going to the gym in the morning before college. The nearest good gym is a 15 minute walk from the house and she would be going at 6am. You are the first one to respond to this news. You are telling Ritu why this is not a good idea and what can happen to girls who are out alone in the early morning and what she should do instead and what happened to a girl in your neighbourhood three years ago who used to go out early.
